description: Node-level TCP, CPU scheduling, and pressure diagnostics for diagnosing collective stalls

panels.description: TCP retransmissions per second per node. A single retransmit on the gloo allreduce path adds ~200ms+ (RTO) of stall.
panels.title: TCP Retransmissions (Decode)

panels.description: TCP retransmissions per second per node. A single retransmit on the gloo allreduce path adds ~200ms+ (RTO) of stall.
panels.title: TCP Retransmissions (Prefill)

panels.description: TCP RTO timeouts per second. Each timeout stalls the connection for at least 200ms.
panels.title: TCP Timeouts (Decode)

panels.description: TCP RTO timeouts per second. Each timeout stalls the connection for at least 200ms.
panels.title: TCP Timeouts (Prefill)

panels.description: Network packet drops on transmit and receive per interface.
panels.title: Network Packet Drops (Decode)

panels.description: Network packet drops on transmit and receive per interface.
panels.title: Network Packet Drops (Prefill)

panels.description: TCP retransmit ratio — retransmitted segments as a fraction of total sent segments. High ratio suggests systematic network issues, not just volume.
panels.title: TCP Retransmit Ratio (Decode)

panels.description: TCP retransmit ratio — retransmitted segments as a fraction of total sent segments. High ratio suggests systematic network issues, not just volume.
panels.title: TCP Retransmit Ratio (Prefill)
RTO Timeouts (Decode)
panels.description: Full RTO timeouts — each one stalls the connection for 200ms+ minimum. This is the metric that matters for tail latency.

panels.title: RTO Timeouts (Prefill)
panels.description: Full RTO timeouts — each one stalls the connection for 200ms+ minimum. This is the metric that matters for tail latency.

panels.title: TLP Probes & Recovery (Decode)
panels.description: Tail Loss Probes sent vs recovered without RTO. High probe count with low recovery means TLP is failing to prevent timeouts.

panels.title: TLP Probes & Recovery (Prefill)
panels.description: Tail Loss Probes sent vs recovered without RTO. High probe count with low recovery means TLP is failing to prevent timeouts.

panels.title: Fast Retransmits (Decode)
panels.description: Fast retransmits (dup-ACK triggered) and SACK recovery episodes. These cost ~1 RTT, not 200ms — mostly benign on a cluster network.

panels.title: Fast Retransmits (Prefill)
panels.description: Fast retransmits (dup-ACK triggered) and SACK recovery episodes. These cost ~1 RTT, not 200ms — mostly benign on a cluster network.
CPU Pressure Stall Information — fraction of time tasks are waiting for CPU. Directly measures CPU contention experienced by workloads.
panels.title: CPU Pressure (PSI Waiting) (Decode)

panels.description: CPU Pressure Stall Information — fraction of time tasks are waiting for CPU. Directly measures CPU contention experienced by workloads.
panels.title: CPU Pressure (PSI Waiting) (Prefill)

panels.description: Per-CPU scheduler run queue wait time — how long threads sit in the run queue before being scheduled.
panels.title: Scheduler Run Queue Wait (Decode)

panels.description: Per-CPU scheduler run queue wait time — how long threads sit in the run queue before being scheduled.
panels.title: Scheduler Run Queue Wait (Prefill)

panels.description: CFS throttled time per second for decode/prefill containers. If containers have CPU limits, CFS throttling on one rank stalls the entire collective.
panels.title: CFS Throttled Time (Decode)

panels.description: CFS throttled time per second for decode/prefill containers. If containers have CPU limits, CFS throttling on one rank stalls the entire collective.
panels.title: CFS Throttled Time (Prefill)

panels.description: Fraction of CFS periods that were throttled. >0.5 means the container is hitting its CPU limit more than half the time.
panels.title: CFS Throttled Period Ratio (Decode)

panels.description: Fraction of CFS periods that were throttled. >0.5 means the container is hitting its CPU limit more than half the time.
panels.title: CFS Throttled Period Ratio (Prefill)

title: Node Diagnostics (TCP / CPU / CFS)
